title: ICE Agent Christian Castro Arrested in Texas; DHS Calls It 'Political Stunt'
excerpt: ICE agent Christian Castro, charged with second-degree assault and falsely reporting a crime in connection with the January 14 shooting of a Venezuelan man in Minneapolis, was arrested in Harlingen, Texas, by the Texas Rangers and Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension. DHS denounced the arrest as "unlawful" while Minnesota officials called it a critical step toward accountability.
content:
On May 29, 2026, ICE agent Christian Castro, 52, was taken into custody in Harlingen, Texas — eleven days after Hennepin County prosecutors charged him with four counts of second-degree assault and one count of falsely reporting a crime.

  • Castro is accused of firing a gunshot through the front door of a Minneapolis home on January 14, 2026, striking Venezuelan national Julio Sosa-Celis in the thigh.
  • DHS had initially supported Castro's account — that he fired in self-defense after being attacked with a shovel and broom — but video evidence directly contradicted that claim. Federal charges against the two Venezuelan men were subsequently dropped.
  • ICE Director Todd Lyons acknowledged in February that two agents had made "untruthful statements" under oath about the incident and placed them on administrative leave.
  • Castro was tracked to Cameron County, Texas by the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension and arrested with assistance from the Texas Rangers.
  • DHS called the state prosecution "unlawful and nothing more than a political stunt," insisting the matter should be handled federally. A Minnesota judge set bail at $200,000.

Castro is the second ICE agent to face criminal prosecution from Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty arising from Operation Metro Surge. If convicted of the most serious charge, he faces up to seven years in prison per count. Extradition proceedings to Minnesota were underway as of early June 2026.
mediaPlaceholder: Booking photo of ICE agent Christian Castro following his arrest in Cameron County, Texas
